topic: FaceTime with Haboba — she asked about marriage again

turns.content: just got off a facetime with my grandmother and I need to debrief because wallah that woman does not miss
Ha! What did she hit you with this time?

it started normal — how's work, are you eating enough, her neighbour got a new cat. classic Haboba content. and then out of NOWHERE she goes "so, any news?" with this little smile. I KNOW what any news means. it means are you seeing anyone. it means when are you getting married

The classic ambush disguised as small talk. "Any news" is doing a lot of heavy lifting in that sentence. Grandmothers worldwide have perfected the art of asking about marriage while maintaining plausible deniability.

How did you handle it?

I panicked and said I got a promotion which isn't even true lol. she was happy for like 30 seconds and then goes "that's wonderful habibi but a career doesn't keep you warm at night." then she told me about her friend's grandson who is a doctor and "very handsome mashallah." and when I pushed back she said she married my grandfather after meeting him twice and they were happy for 47 years. I mean. what do you say to that

You can't argue with a 47-year track record — she's got the data on her side. "A career doesn't keep you warm at night" is an all-time grandmother line. Delivered with love, received with internal screaming.

Does she do this every call or is it seasonal?

every. single. time. I think she has a calendar reminder tbh. but I can't even be mad because she says it with so much love?? like she genuinely wants me to be happy. she ended the call by telling me to eat more lentils for my skin and blowing me like 15 kisses. so even after the marriage interrogation I hung up smiling. she's the best honestly

Fifteen kisses and a lentil prescription — she covered your love life, your career, your nutrition, and your skincare in a single FaceTime. That's efficient grandmothering. Are you going to eat the lentils?

lol MAYBE. I do like lentil soup actually. okay fine yes I'll make some this week. wallah she has a 100% success rate in getting me to do things. anyway thanks for letting me debrief haha I needed to laugh about it
Haboba: undefeated. Enjoy the lentil soup — and next time she asks about "any news," just tell her you've been eating your lentils. Grandmothers love compliance. Have a good night!
